i have a standard flat panel monitor in the laptop, 1280x800. i also have a dell 2407 wfp monitor (lovely thing it is)

i would like it so that when the monitor is plugged in, it outputs to the monitor and the monitor only, at 1920x1200, and when it's not plugged in it should display at 1280x800 on the laptop screen. or any set up vaguely resembling that is fine

as it stands, whenever i log out, it goes to 800x600. i can set it to 1280x800 manually but that's a hassle. i can't get the monitor to work at all really. i am at my wits end

please help!

(i never had any of this **** with 7.04 btw... and the xorg.conf was a hell of a lot more readable back then too)

### Post by schneider707 on 2007-10-18
> **Choad said:**
> i cant even install nvidia-settings without removing the new nvidia driver. i am not sure if that is going to break the system or not :( 

As far as I know, it won't "break your system." However, removing the driver will cause X to shut itself down forcing you to run the dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg to get X back up and running. It takes all about 4 min to fix the situation so don't worry about some ungodly down time.

---

### Post by bigb2007 on 2007-10-18
Wouldn't you need to use metamodes to do this?  I run a similar setup, dell 9400 laptop(1440x900), and an attached external 1900x1200 display.  My xorg looks like this : 

```

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Wed Sep 12 14:30:30 PDT 2007

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    RgbPath         "/usr/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "DCL9A"
    HorizSync       24.0 - 80.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 75.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce Go 7900 GS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "metamodes" "CRT: nvidia-auto-select +1440+0, DFP: nvidia-auto-select +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```

Though this will put you in multi-head mode, with your laptop as the secondary.  Works pretty well.  When I pull the external monitor off, the screen switches to the laptop, and all is well.  I'm posting from the laptop now, and it was dual-screen'd when I was at work(I use my external monitor as a primary at work).

ah well, it's certainly an improvment!

what's the deal with separate x screens? how would i go about setting that up? could i have it so that my laptop runs at 1280x800 and my monitor at 1920x1200 without all that space between the laptop and the desktop?

one sec ill print screen to show you what i mean

[[IMG]http://img401.imageshack.us/img401/1964/screenshotwj8.th.jpg[/IMG]](http://img401.imageshack.us/my.php?image=screenshotwj8.jpg)

all that space is "dead". i can put windows in it, but i can't see them! could get confusing! the mouse has to travel through it to get to the laptop too. also, icons on the desktop get placed top left of the laptop screen, not the big monitor (which is odd because the panel and everything is on the monitor, so it knows it is my "primary" screen) any suggestions? ill post my xorg.conf (recently reset with dpkg-reconfigure because the gui app made a mess of it completely.

```

# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Nvidia 7600"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Laptop Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-75
	VertRefresh	30-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Nvidia 7600"
	Monitor		"Laptop Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	Option		"TwinView"	"1"
	Option		"metamodes"	"CRT: nvidia-auto-select +1920+0, DFP: nvidia-auto-select +0+0"
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1920x1200" "1280x800"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"

# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
```

### Post by burningbed on 2007-10-21
I have had somewhat similar problems (see [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=583642]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=583642")) and they were all solved by uninstalling xserver-xgl.
I'd try running running apt-get remove xserver-xgl and see if it helps.

---
